Hey guys, welcome to Theme Plate 11. This is the upgraded FAQ and this really is a staple in just about every launch campaign. So feel free to use this as part of your minimum viable funnel. And the FAQ is traditionally used to counter objections, but that's not all it can do. It's actually capable of significantly more. And when done right, the upgraded FAQ actually has the power to counter objections, position you against competitors, reinforce the big ahas that you instill during the pre-launch and create safety by prepaving the outcome while also helping them identify as a buyer all without breaking form. So it's pulling a lot of weight. It's really doing its work. And I usually like to roll this email out about 48 hours before the close. In other words, it's that last email we would we will send it during the sale sequence before getting into the closing sequence, which you'll learn in module seven. So the general format is this, you know, you do want to lead in and frame those questions or even skepticism as positive traits that are in line with being a buyer. And then the FAQ, this is once again, a flexible theme plate. Don't try to make it too rigid and try to shoehorn your copy and your words into this. But I usually like to reference this model here as the eight types of questions that I will use. So objection one, so question one, sorry, will be the biggest objection and transform it into a big opportunity. So a little bit of kind of like objection alchemy there, if you will. Objection two, question two, sorry, will take another objection and use it to point to a specific and unique aspect of your product that addresses it. And then question three, will bust one of those major industry wide myths, those sacred cows. So just really reminding them of that. So that's how you start recontextualizing and bolstering those sales arguments instead of diluting them. For I like to positioning gains competitors and really get buy-in from my prospect on my unique way of doing things. So this is very much a positioning thing. This is still trying to keep them in the frame of, you know, solution aware now, because they have already been like symptomally, symptomatically diagnosed, they've gotten a real problem. And now they're bridging into solution aware, but we want to make sure that ours is the only logical solution. So this is where it's important to start positioning against your competitors. And then we're going to use it question five, sometimes as a springboard into your fuller USP or unique selling proposition, you'll see how that plays out in just a bit. Question six, this is pretty universal, the time objection, you know, will I have time to do this? Will I have time to learn French? Will I have time to play guitar? Will I have time to create my branding in my website? Will I have time, time, time? So that's just kind of one of those universal objections. And this will be your chance to work that one in. And I mean, one thing that's been consistent across this about everything I've done is, you know, people aren't afraid of spending time, they're afraid of wasting time. So prove to them that they could get a return on time that every second, every minute they invest into your program, into your training is going to have that payoff. That's what they need to hear here at this point. They don't need you to say it's only going to be an hour a week or 20 minutes per week or five hours a week or whatever it takes. They don't need that kind of data. They just need to know that what they do invest, whether it is 10 minutes, five hours, whatever it is, is going to have a payoff. And then you could actually springboard into the offer while keeping frame within the Q&A itself, and then use your risk reversal, your guarantees as well, like to really close and wrap it up. And these are elements that you're going to see are more prominent in the closing sequence. But since this email, this theme played in particular is kind of like on the border of the close, we do bring in some of that risk reversal and guarantee stuff. So that is it. And here is kind of like, that's kind of the general format and the theme plate, the organizing container for it looks a little something like this. So we will intro describing your reader, but making it sound like you're just really describing your best prospect or not sound like you're simply describing your prospect, but actually describing your best prospect. If you've had calls with customers as soon as they've come in and asked them what made you join, you know, what objections did you overcome? If you've gotten those testimonials, use that, you know, and go deeper into those conversations, because you do want to make this as accurate as possible to your real buyers and your best prospects. Validate the reader, tell them you appreciate that skepticism is a good thing, validate it and meet it in their current context of where they're at instead of resisting and rejecting that. Welcome it skepticism is a good thing. And then why you appreciate that person connected to the product you're selling, why that might make them a good fit, why that might qualify them to be perfect for this program or product. And then tell them what you're about to tell them. You know, this is when you're just going to kind of transition and jump into your FAQ, then we're going to go into the Q&As. You could choose those eight variations of it that we looked at above and repeat that for as many as you want to work with being tonal where appropriate. And you're just going to organize those questions almost like a sales page. So you're going to open as we showed with more of those problem and objection related questions and finishing with the closing ones. Like what happens exactly, you know, when they come into the program, what they'll receive, you know, if they need help ordering and all those risk reducers. So it kind of does function like sales page in that respect. And then you'll have your sign off, you could come back to add some urgency, you know, if you're closing in 48 hours. Once again, you don't want to ramp it up if you still have more than 48 hours left, followed by your final call to action. So that is this theme plate in a nutshell. I'm going to run through an example that we actually did for courses that convert questions. People ask before saying yes to courses that convert. You know, we had a lot of data, we had a lot of information. So it was kind of easy to pull this out. But you could describe what your best customers have been asking. So that's really what we're aiming for here. The business owners who get the most out of my programs are usually the ones who ask the most questions. So making that in line with the buyer's journey, you know, questions aren't a bad thing. The ones who refuse to let me off the hook, the ones who hold my feet to the fire and dare to ask the hard nose questions. Personally, I love it. So we're inviting it. We're showing we're not phased by that. Because at the end of the day, I only want you in courses that convert if you know without a doubt that it's the missing link between you and a fully leveraged and profitable online business. So linking it to the outcome they want to achieve. So without further ado, here are the most common questions I've been fielding over the last few days. If you're still on the fence, chances are at least one or two of them has been wreaking havoc on your sleep patterns. And this is kind of specific to you know, during a launch, you know, when, you know, this new program is out and it's starting to get into those 48 hours, I actually have lost sleep, you know, so that's not super untypical. Sorry about that. Let's jump in. And then we dive into the questions, you know, some of those bigger concerns, like what if there are other courses on my topic. And this was one of the bigger objections or sacred cows about their ability to succeed. So awesome. That means there's a market and an opportunity. Because where there's competition, there are also dissatisfied customers who didn't get their problem solved the first time. So biggest objection into a biggest opportunity and how to do it. Your job is to discover how current courses have let them down, note the failure points and create a course that rescues them from those issues and helps them achieve the results they're looking for. Second objection, what if I don't have a list? Sure, it would be awesome to go into it with an active list, but your success certainly won't depend on it. There's a crazy myth that you need to have lists of subscribers that trust you enough to tell you what they want. But in reality, that's just one of many validation strategies. So busting that myth and then pointing to an element of your program that solves it. In module one, you'll learn how to gauge market interest and create a course that your market wants, even if you don't have an email list to send surveys to yet. I call it the minimum viable validation method, and it's a perfect way to get up and running fast. No list needed. So we just took second biggest objection that customers were experiencing and linked it to a valuable and important part of the course. How long do I need to be creating free content, etc. before I create my course? I bet you've heard something along the lines of you need to give free valuable content for six, nine, 12 months before you've earned the right to create a course. Where does this even come from? As long as there's a problem, your target audience experiences that you're uniquely equipped to solve. It doesn't matter if you've been offering free content for 10 years or 10 days. Yes, being in the game longer may give you more insights into what the market wants, but these aspects can easily be overcome with the strategies you'll learn. In short, you don't need permission. You need proper strategy and implementation. So we took another thing that was extremely disempowering to them, this myth that's been holding them back, and we disarmed it, while re-contextualizing it with our offer. So this is something we would have talked about in the pre-launch or the beginning of the webinar, which serves as a pre-launch, but now in the sales sequence, we're re-contextualizing it within the realm of the offer. I've seen other course building programs that also go deep into the marketing side, as courses that convert do it. So this is all about positioning. And in this case, we could take something that would be seen as negative, such as not including too many modules on the marketing and promotional side, and instead turn that into a legitimate positive, that actually allows them to maintain 100% of focus on the task at hand and allowing them to finish building an asset. And this is huge. This was a game changer for many students, because one of the things that derails progress is trying to market it as you build it and just wearing too many hats at the same time. So this course really focused its USP on creating that asset as quickly and as powerfully as possible and creating something that is designed to be able to sell later, because you've gone through all the right steps. So that is number four, linking it to the USP. Conversion is a two-sided process. Yes, marketing funnels and promotional strategies are sexy, but as I'm sure you've experienced, it also gets overwhelming and leads you down an endless rabbit hole of delay and distraction. I want to remind you that what you're doing inside courses that convert is building a real profitable asset that can generate income for the rest of your life. It's a process that requires laser focus, meticulous, step-by-step detail, and a disciplined approach that backburns all tactics that don't serve the immediate goal of building your course. I know this will turn off the shiny object seekers, but that's okay. My obligation is into them. It's to you to driven focus on entrepreneur who's made the big decision to build a valuable long-term asset with as little distraction as possible. So here you're just re-qualifying your prospect and affirming your way of doing things versus your competitors. And if you do have a slight case of funnel fever, don't we all? You'll get access to a very special bonus course launch master plan. So we do satisfy that itch without overwhelming them with scratching. We don't let them derail their progress and a true outcome of the course. Then we have that universal time objection. Will I really have time to build my own online course? In short, the busier you are, the more you need a leveraged freedom building, profit generating online course in your business. So it's always good to ask, is there a catch 22 here? If your audience is time starved, does that mean they need what you're selling even more? Yeah. So that's something that is going to be applicable across a broad, broad range of topics and courses. And I get it. You're a super busy entrepreneur with a million things and maybe even a day job on your plate. That's why courses that convert was built with one thing in mind, implementation. We've laid out all the steps in crystal clear detail so you don't have to worry about being your own project manager. Instead, you just have to block off a couple hours a week, dive in and follow the step by step process as the guide you towards your first profitable online course. Once again, people aren't afraid of spending time. They're afraid of not getting a return on that time. So just by encouraging them that they get to plug themselves into this system and that everything they're working in it is going to lead to that result, that is encouraging. That's what the audience needs to hear for the time objection. A lot of people would have just stopped, no, you could just get started with like four hours a week and you'll be fine. But it's not the time they're concerned about. It's the return on that time. So I really invite you to explore how return on time would apply to you, your course and your audience. How many hours will I need to invest every week? So this is just a continuation of that, getting a little more concrete. Once you enroll, you'll have instant access. I'd say a minimum you should be ready to invest is two to three hours. Small investment considering you're building an asset that could be worth more than most people's home. So this is just kind of a nice little metaphor that ties it into the value of what they're really building here. And whenever you do mention how many hours they are going to be spending, it's great to contrast that to anchor that and it's the value that they're going to receive from that investment. Then we are getting into those more closing elements. Can you remind me of what I get when I enroll today? And then we go through that walkthrough. So this could be similar to that Aladdin style we did in the launch sequence. But yeah, you don't have to go module by module. You could say the full program and just really do that in a general way, outline the bonuses, really kind of show what they're getting. So that operates kind of as that mini sales page. And then getting into the risk reversal. So is it really risk-free? Give your guarantee. Really kind of take away all anxiety around that. And with your close, if that road leads you to courses at Convert, then I look forward to meeting on the in side. So that is the upgraded FAQ. In a nutshell, what we're doing here is we are really framing our questions both one as a positive thing that's in line with being a buyer. These are legitimately questions that people asked before buying the program. So we create that identity with being a buyer. And then we really walk them through a lot of the elements that a sales page would, starting with those more surface level objections, tying that back to unique aspects of your product, making those objections opportunities, and really just kind of walking them through that process, holding their hand and just bring them deeper and deeper towards that close. So that is it. This is one of those core emails I'll turn to over and over again across pretty much every launch. So I invite you to spend some time with it. Don't get too hung up on the order of the questions. Just follow that general path that we outlined here. But there is room for some flexibility here. So don't get too hung up once again and trying to shoehorn it and make it follow that exact format. Just make sure that you're using kind of that general orientation around it and that you're hitting the beats that we're really trying to coach here. So that's it. I will take a break and I'll meet you in theme plate number 12.
